methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 15 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $7.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Destinus is a late-stage frontier-tech company operating in Defense & Security, having raised over $400M to reach a current post-money valuation of $1.5B. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 15 same-sector peers across similar valuation tiers, supplemented by public market references (2 public comps) and established private benchmarks including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, benchmarking typical late-stage to next-stage advancement, applies a 1.2x multiplier to the $1.5B base, yielding $1.8B. Peer-median analysis across the 15-company comp set (range $300M–$7.5B, median $1.4B) implies a $1.4B valuation, anchoring to similarly-positioned Defense & Security firms. The two methods produced meaningfully aligned results, with only a $400M spread between implied values.

Triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.6B. This range encompasses the current $1.5B post-money valuation at the lower end. Key uncertainties include stage-specific timing (when next financing event occurs) and peer comparability—the comp set exhibits wide dispersion ($7.5B span), reflecting heterogeneity in Defense & Security business models, capital intensity, and commercial traction. Further clarity on Destinus's specific revenue scale and customer concentration would improve precision.
